decisions.decision: TASK-026 (plugin chain P6: MCP task-store server) closed APPROVE — auto-closed per standing authorization. CLEANEST review of the whole chain (0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 0 LOW / 1 NIT). Researcher mcp-server skill + KB proposal; tests-first bb1a663; impl 2447977. 🎉 PLUGIN CHAIN (epic TASK-020) NOW 100% COMPLETE — all 7 tickets P1-P7 closed & reviewer-APPROVED. The framework is a fully-packaged, installable, versioned (0.1.0) Claude Code plugin with an MCP broaden-later seam.
decisions.rationale: P6 wraps src/task-store.js as six MCP tools (list_todos/list_ready/get_task/create_task/transition_status/append_comment) over a stdio server, bundled via the same esbuild pattern as the other entrypoints (dist/mcp-server.cjs, SDK+zod inlined as devDeps, no runtime deps). It's the 'broaden later' seam: a non-Claude-Code MCP client gets ticket CRUD but NOT the orchestrator/subagent loop (Claude Code-exclusive), documented in the WILL/WON'T docstring (AC4). The two reviewer-scrutinized risk items came back SOUND: (1) get_task's /^TASK-\d{3,}$/ key guard runs before any path join, defeating traversal (../../etc/passwd etc. rejected; well-formed-absent → null); (2) zero stdout writes (only stderr) so the JSON-RPC channel stays clean. AC3 round-trips through a REAL SDK Client over InMemoryTransport — deterministic, automatable, not a stub. Re-adding plugin.json's mcpServers './.mcp.json' (the key P7 deliberately removed when the file didn't exist) closes the loop the publish-config guard was protecting; `claude plugin validate .` PASSES. The researcher correctly judged the MCP SDK substantial enough to warrant a reusable skill (Atlassian migration + future MCP work) — created byte-identical in .claude/skills/ + plugin-root skills/, and the tool surface deliberately mirrors Jira field names so TASK-003's eventual backend swap is loss-free. Only the live `claude plugin install`→`details` MCP-servers(1) sensor + the TASK-027 AC1 live agent-loop E2E remain — both inherently manual, run at install time, NOT code blockers. Suite now 339 passed / 4 skipped. Open: TASK-003 (Jira mig, MCP-blocked, no motion). Natural session-end / milestone point.

decisions.decision: TASK-027 (plugin chain P7: E2E + quickstart + publish prep) closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS — auto-closed per standing authorization (green review, 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM). Tests-first 9236e99, impl 2b0be23. PLUGIN CHAIN MILESTONE: P1-P5 + P7 all DONE; only P6 (TASK-026 MCP, the deferrable broaden-later seam) remains open. Two human publish decisions resolved this session: this repo IS the marketplace (source './'); first release pinned at semver 0.1.0. Doing P7 before P6 was a human-directed authorized deviation; it surfaced and fixed a real latent bug (dangling mcpServers ref).
decisions.rationale: AC2/AC3/AC4 fully met and locked by a deterministic 317/317 (3 skipped) suite across two reviewer runs. The capstone's automatable surface is proven: publish config (version 0.1.0, marketplace name/source), the make-template publish-scrub regression lock (TASK-019 guarantee re-pinned at the P7 layer), and the non-technical plugin-install quickstart (added WITHOUT removing the clone workflow that readme.spec.js + make-template AC7 pin). The load-bearing discovery: doing P7 before P6 exposed that plugin.json's `mcpServers: './.mcp.json'` pointed at a file that won't exist until P6 — a plausible `claude plugin validate`/install failure that would have blocked real installs. Removing the dangling key (P6 re-adds it with the actual .mcp.json) made `claude plugin validate` PASS (dev ran it, CLI v2.1.156: marketplace + plugin.json both pass, no mcpServers error). The two TASK-021 plugin-scaffold assertion flips (version-unset→pinned, mcpServers-declared→unset-until-P6) were reviewer-confirmed LEGITIMATE supersessions anticipated by the manifest's own comments, with the strict version contract living in publish-config.spec.js so net coverage rose — same authorized-supersession pattern as commit f456114. AC1 (live clean-machine E2E: install → /init-project → drive a task through the agent loop, with observations recorded) is the one genuinely-manual item: its automatable surface is covered and `claude plugin validate` passed, but the live agent-loop drive needs an interactive human/clean-machine session and is recorded as the remaining operational follow-up — consistent with how the whole chain treated live `claude plugin` sensors (it.skip). 1 LOW (that pending live run) + 1 NIT (cosmetic semver assertion shape) deferred without a follow-up ticket. The framework is now an installable, versioned (0.1.0), self-bootstrapping Claude Code plugin; P6 adds the optional MCP broaden-later seam.

decisions.decision: TASK-025 (plugin chain P5: orchestrator ship + activation) closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS — auto-closed per standing authorization (green review, 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM). Tests-first 5d5c403, impl 715abc4. This was the spike's §H flagged HIGHEST-RISK ticket and it landed clean. P1-P5 of the 7-ticket chain now done. Remaining: P6 (TASK-026 MCP server, DEFERRABLE branch) and P7 (TASK-027 E2E + quickstart + publish decisions, critical-path finale). Reached a sequencing decision point — surfacing to the human rather than auto-picking P6 vs P7.
decisions.rationale: All 4 ACs met with a deterministic 298/298 (2 skipped) suite across two reviewer runs and a reproducible dist bundle. The load-bearing correctness win is AC4: both orchestrator.md copies (dev + plugin-root, byte-identical per parity) were reconciled from the stale v1 single-file session model to the v2 pointer+bundle model and cross-checked consistent with CLAUDE.md + state/README.md + session.schema.json — shipping the stale text would have propagated the wrong session contract into every user project. AC1/AC2 honor the human-locked policy (prompt-once-then-marker-merge, never clobber): mergeRoutingBlock preserves user bytes outside the markers and is idempotent on re-run. The no-TTY consent nuance (a slash command can't drive readline) was resolved by making CLAUDE.md-routing consent a SEPARATE channel from wizard intake answers — an explicit --claude-md-consent flag / claude_md_consent key in answers-mode (no prompt), prompt-once in interactive mode. The reviewer specifically cleared the consent-swallow design (write is outside the try/catch; production prompter null) and the orchestrator.md consistency. AC3 ships an always-on backstop skill so the orchestrator activates even pre-init / pre-CLAUDE.md. 2 LOWs (adversarial-marker robustness, broad consent catch) + 1 NIT deferred without a follow-up ticket per the chain close pattern — none reachable by the framework's own output. Why pause for the human now: 'roll to next' is ambiguous at this branch point — P6 is explicitly the deferrable broaden-later seam, while P7 is the critical-path finale that REQUIRES human decisions (marketplace hosting/name, explicit version vs commit-SHA) that the spike deliberately parked. Also surfaced a latent issue: plugin.json's mcpServers points at ./.mcp.json which does not yet exist (P6 territory) and may make `claude plugin validate` complain. Better to let the human choose the path and settle the publish questions than guess.

decisions.decision: TASK-024 (plugin chain P4) closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS — auto-closed per standing plugin-chain authorization (green review, 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM). Tests-first de5c58f, impl 46a8478. The /init-project command + non-interactive answers-mode are the user-facing bootstrap: `/agentic-framework:init-project` gathers intake conversationally, writes an answers JSON, and runs the shipped bundle against the user's project. Chain critical path now P5(025)→P7(027) remaining; P6(026, MCP) still the deferrable branch.
decisions.rationale: Cleanest review profile of the chain so far: 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 1 LOW / 1 NIT, 270/270 passed (2 skipped) DETERMINISTIC across two clean reviewer runs, and the committed dist/init.cjs proven == fresh `npm run build:plugin` output (empty git diff) so the shipped bundle can't drift from source. The load-bearing design call was the answers-mode seam: a Claude Code slash command runs through the Bash tool which has NO interactive TTY, so the interactive readline prompter is unusable from a command — the fix is an `answers` object that bypasses runQuestionnaire entirely (prompter provably never called, asserted by a throwing test prompter) plus a `--answers-file` CLI flag the command body invokes. Idempotency preserved by construction: already_initialized short-circuits before any write even when answers are supplied, so a re-run can't clobber PROJECT.md or duplicate the seed backlog. Up-front validateSuppliedAnswers prevents half-materialization on malformed input. The 1 LOW (implicit resumed-branch archive-skip asymmetry) + 1 NIT (type-specific keys advisory not validated) deferred without a follow-up ticket per the chain close pattern. The §D.5 live `claude plugin details` registration proof + AC4 fresh-install cache-not-mutated assertion remain the deliberately-skipped manual CLI sensor (it.skip, same as TASK-021 scaffold) — an operational step run at install time, explicitly NOT a blocker for the code close. Net: P1-P4 of the 7-ticket chain are done; the framework is one command away from bootstrapping an arbitrary user project, pending P5 orchestrator activation (the generated/merged project CLAUDE.md that actually wires the orchestrator into that project).

decisions.decision: TASK-020 spike closed (done, doc committed bd252d3). Minted the 7-ticket plugin packaging chain TASK-021..027 from the spike's §F. Three human decisions locked into the chain: (1) mint all 7 phases; (2) EXCLUDE bin/make-template.js from the shipped plugin bin/ (publish-time dev script only) — baked into TASK-021 AC4; (3) /init-project writes the project CLAUDE.md routing via PROMPT-ONCE-then-marker-merge, never silent/clobber — baked into TASK-025 ACs 1-2. Chain critical path: P1(021)→P2(022)→P3(023)→P4(024)→P5(025)→P7(027); P6(026, MCP) branches off P3 and is the deferrable 'broaden later' seam.
decisions.rationale: Spike delivered all 6 ACs with the retargeting blast radius shown to be small (3 bin/ shells + 1 dep-location bug; all src/ already repoRoot-clean). AC4 proof-of-load executed by the orchestrator as a sensor: validate/install/details/uninstall via a relative-path local marketplace, agent+command registered, zero residue — and the `claude plugin` CLI is non-interactive so it's reusable for chain verification. Two load-bearing findings shaped the chain: (a) a plugin-root CLAUDE.md is NOT loaded as context, forcing orchestrator activation via a generated/merged project CLAUDE.md + backstop skill (TASK-025); (b) agents/orchestrator.md still documents the v1 single-file session model and MUST be reconciled before shipping or every user project inherits the wrong contract (TASK-025 AC4 — flagged by the spike as the highest-risk correctness item). Dev-repo self-use preservation (don't break the agents we build WITH while relocating them to plugin layout) baked into TASK-021 AC5. Deferred publish decisions (marketplace hosting/name, explicit version) parked in TASK-027 to be resolved with implementation context rather than guessed up front. (Extends the 2026-05-28T22:10 plugin-direction decision below.)

decisions.decision: New strategic direction: distribute the framework as a Claude Code PLUGIN (primary vehicle), with a bundled MCP server designed in as the seam to broaden to non-Code clients later. Target surface chosen by the human: 'devs now, broaden later'. Spawned TASK-020 as an architecture SPIKE (not impl) to produce the design + a phased impl ticket chain before any plugin code is written.
decisions.rationale: Verdict was MCP is the WRONG primary vehicle: claude-code-guide confirmed (cited docs) that the Agent-tool subagent orchestration — the framework's core value — is exclusive to Claude Code and CANNOT be carried by an MCP server (subagents are file-based .claude/agents/ constructs). Plugins, by contrast, bundle agents+skills+commands+hooks+MCP as one `/plugin install`-able, versioned, auto-discovered unit — mapping almost exactly onto the human's 'install and go' vision. Corrected two overclaims in the human's framing: there is nothing to 'train' (agents are prompt definitions; agent-generator already CONFIGURES them per project type), and the framework gets a user 'ready to develop anything' (scaffolded + configured + backlog-seeded + orchestrated with human-in-the-loop gates) — NOT a one-shot autonomous product generator, by deliberate design. Sequenced as a spike because repoRoot-retargeting (today everything assumes the framework's own repo is the project; as a plugin it must operate on the user's project) and orchestrator ship/activation in an arbitrary project are genuine unknowns that must be designed before minting impl tickets. Most src/ functions already take repoRoot as a param, so retargeting is tractable repackaging rather than a rewrite — but the CLI shells, state/+tasks/ layout, and CLAUDE.md routing co-location are the open questions the spike resolves.

decisions.decision: TASK-019 closed APPROVE on first review pass (human-authorized close per principle #4). New bin/make-template.js delivers a safe-by-default upstream template-prep command — fixes clone distribution residue at the source rather than leaning on the runtime archiveFrameworkHistory safety net. Spawned from a live end-to-end clone test (clone → npm install → node bin/init.js) that proved the created-branch flow works but leaves two pieces of residue the runtime archiver does not clean: (1) TASK-* .md sidecars survive (archiver regex only matches TASK-NNN.json), (2) the dev session bundle lingers in state/sessions/. Out-of-scope runtime-archiver hardening deliberately NOT folded in — noted in the ticket description for a possible follow-up to keep this ticket single-concern.
decisions.rationale: Cleanest possible review: 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 0 LOW / 2 cosmetic NITs, 206/206 vitest specs deterministic across two reviewer runs. Two-commit cadence preserved tests-first discipline (0d7ce87 test = 15 failing specs across all 7 ACs; 153a8f0 impl = bin/make-template.js + README 'Preparing a distribution' note; git diff over tests/ between them is empty). Key design wins the reviewer verified: (a) computeTargets() is the single source of truth — the dry-run plan and the apply loop consume the same arrays so the printed plan can never drift from what --yes executes; (b) apply:false performs ZERO filesystem mutation (early return + byte-identity snapshot test), so a careless invocation cannot destroy the live dev repo — the central safety guarantee of a destructive tool; (c) both JSON rewrites are exact-byte via atomicWriteFile. The 2 NITs (TASK_RESIDUE_RE=/^TASK-/ breadth vs the archiver's stricter pattern — safe because tasks/ ships empty; README prose softer than naming base-agent files) deferred without a follow-up ticket per the TASK-013/015/016 close pattern. Net new tests +15 (191 → 206). Also corrected an earlier orchestrator error in-flight: init is NOT runnable on a bare clone (dep chain backlog-seeder → task-store → ajv needs npm install) — now documented in the README via AC7.

decisions.decision: Plan session management as TASK-004 ahead of TASK-001. Augment Claude Code's native transcript handling rather than replace it; introduce portable per-session bundles under state/sessions/<id>/ and a portable knowledge/ lessons-learned KB consulted by the Researcher before web research.
decisions.rationale: User needs cross-machine session portability and cross-project lesson reuse. Building this before the task-store wiring (TASK-001) means every subsequent task immediately benefits from continuity + KB lookups.

decisions.decision: Keep state-of-record as files (JSON + Markdown) rather than migrating to a SQL or NoSQL database for session/KB storage.
decisions.rationale: User proposed a DB-backed store for stability and SQL sort/query benefits. Rejected because the stated #1 requirement was cross-machine portability + inspectability; a directory of JSON+MD wins on zip-and-email portability, git diff visibility, and zero-install inspection. The query-performance gains from SQL don't apply at current scale (0 closed sessions, 0 KB entries). If the KB grows past ~200 entries an opt-in SQLite-as-derived-index can be added later, with the .md files remaining source of truth.

decisions.decision: Resolved all 10 open design questions for TASK-004 (full text in tasks/TASK-004.json comment dated 2026-05-24). Two deviations from researcher recommendations: (a) transcript snapshot taken on every pause + end (not end-only); (b) schema-version field unified as `schema_version` in both pointer and bundle (not split as `version`/`schema_version`).
decisions.rationale: Both deviations prioritize user-stated requirements over researcher's cost concerns. (a) Portability + crash recovery > a small amount of extra I/O. (b) Naming consistency for future readers > a refactor cost that is effectively zero since no production code yet consumes the v1 field name.

decisions.decision: Add `atomicWriteFiles` (two-phase commit batched primitive) to src/atomic-write.js rather than inlining the recipe in src/task-store.js.
decisions.rationale: The TASK-001 atomic-write spec requires `max(fsyncSync.callOrder) < min(renameSync.callOrder)` across the whole mutation (task file + index file). Two sequential single-file `atomicWriteFile` calls interleave fsync/rename, violating the invariant. Two options: (a) add a batched primitive to atomic-write.js, (b) duplicate the EBUSY retry loop + tmp-naming in task-store.js. Picked (a) — additive (no existing single-file caller changes), keeps the EBUSY recipe in one place, and gives any future multi-file writer the same semantics for free. Reviewer audited every existing single-file caller (src/bundle.js ×2, src/knowledge.js, src/pointer.js, src/transcript.js) and confirmed zero regression.

decisions.decision: TASK-001 closed APPROVE on the first review pass. 5 LOWs spun out as TASK-009 (hardening) rather than fixed in-band.
decisions.rationale: Reviewer verdict was clean (0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 5 LOW, 2 NIT) and independently re-validated comment shape via ajv round-trip against tasks/schema.json. All 5 LOWs are real edge cases (partial-commit window, TOCTOU, no tmp recovery for tasks/, listTodos ignores depends_on, no defensive ajv on read) but none block correctness today. Spinning them into a dedicated follow-up keeps the TASK-001 close clean and gives the next operator an unambiguous punch list. TASK-008 (TASK-004 LOWs) and TASK-009 (TASK-001 LOWs) can be batched in a single hardening sprint when the team is ready.

decisions.decision: Orchestrator authorized the impl developer to fix a one-line `import Ajv from 'ajv'` -> `'ajv/dist/2020.js'` test infrastructure bug inside the TASK-002 IMPL commit, overriding the standard 'do not edit tests during impl' rule.
decisions.rationale: Phase-1 dev's `created_task_conforms_to_schema` test imported default Ajv (draft-07-only metaschema) against `tasks/schema.json` which declares draft-2020-12. The test couldn't compile the schema regardless of input data — pure test-infra defect with the production code being correct. Three other test files (live-state, pointer, knowledge-files) use plain Ajv and pass because their target schemas omit `$schema` and Ajv defaults to draft-07 implicitly. Bouncing back to phase-1 dev would have been process theater (same single-line fix either way). Pragmatic call: authorize in-band, require the impl commit body to document the override explicitly so the reviewer can argue it. Reviewer confirmed the documentation, the fix as one line, no assertion weakening, and zero regression in other Ajv consumers.

decisions.decision: TASK-002 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. MEDIUM lexical-sort finding rolled into TASK-009 (AC6) rather than spawning a new ticket.
decisions.rationale: Reviewer flagged that `buildIndexBytes`/`listTodos` use string-compare on the key, so `TASK-1000` lexically sorts before `TASK-999` once a 4-digit key exists. Real bug, but pre-existing from TASK-001 (TASK-002's padding test only checked the new key string, not its index position) and only manifests when a 4-digit key lands in the store — which won't happen for hundreds of tickets. Same module, same fix profile (one-line comparator swap on two lines) as the other 5 task-store hardening items already in TASK-009. Rolling in as AC6 keeps the hardening work in one bucket rather than fragmenting it across two follow-up tickets. Two LOWs (CLI AC-count silent coercion, title-whitespace not trimmed) declined without a follow-up — both are minor ergonomic gaps that don't violate any spec, and `tasks/schema.json`'s title minLength is 1 which already accepts whitespace by design.

decisions.decision: Build a project-intake wizard on top of our own primitives instead of adopting GSD wholesale. Front-load with TASK-010 (question engine).
decisions.rationale: User wants the framework to be cloneable by a non-technical operator who is then walked through an intake that configures agents and seeds a backlog. Two paths existed: (a) adopt GSD `/gsd-new-project` and its `.planning/` model, (b) build our own intake reusing what we already have (prompter from bin/new-task.js, atomicWriteFiles, lifecycle bundles for resumability, task store). User picked (b) explicitly because GSD's `.planning/` collides with our `tasks/` + `state/sessions/` model — two state systems would guarantee 'why doesn't this ticket appear' as the first support bug. Project-type taxonomy locked as (A) curated enum {web-saas, cli-tool, data-pipeline, ml-model, library} + 'other' escape hatch that defers to LLM inference; predictable for non-technical users, flexible when it doesn't fit. Six-ticket chain: TASK-010 (engine, this one), TASK-011 (question library + PROJECT.md), TASK-012 (bin/init.js + CLAUDE.md first-chat routing), TASK-013 (agent generator per project type), TASK-014 (backlog seeder from use cases), TASK-015 (UX polish + non-technical README quickstart).

decisions.decision: TASK-010 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. 3-phase engine invocation in bin/new-task.js. MEDIUM dead-code rolled into TASK-015 (the UX polish ticket that will revisit the same question array).
decisions.rationale: Reviewer in fresh context (independent) approved with 0 HIGH / 1 MEDIUM cosmetic / 7 LOW / 2 NIT. 74/74 specs green twice. The MEDIUM (dead-code `validate` on title question that required:true already covers, plus a typo `engineerPrompter`) lives in the same file TASK-015 will touch for UX-polish anyway, so rolling it forward avoids a one-off polish commit and keeps each follow-up ticket self-contained. Phase-2 dev's IMPL commit body was empty — design decisions captured in the TASK-010 close comment instead so the trail survives. The 4 LOW design questions (engine `when` undefined semantics, silent JSON.parse swallow on corrupt resume, no re-validation of restored answers, three-phase invocation breaks cross-phase backtrack) are all correctly answered at the engine layer; TASK-012 wizard is where strict-resume policy can be opt-in.

decisions.decision: TASK-011 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. Per-branch namespacing applied to ALL id collisions (not just the `language` one phase-1 dev flagged). MEDIUM + 4 LOWs rolled into new TASK-016 (PROJECT.md hardening), to be taken BEFORE TASK-013 lands.
decisions.rationale: Phase-2 dev surfaced and applied namespacing to both `language` -> {cli_language, library_language} AND `deployment_target` -> {web_deployment_target, ml_deployment_target}. Defensible call — keeps prompt wording specific per branch, no shared-question complexity. Reviewer's MEDIUM (comma-in-array-item silent corruption) isn't reachable from the wizard happy path (engine's `multi` type splits on `,` itself) but BECOMES reachable when TASK-013 lands a non-wizard caller of writeProjectMd. Rolling it + the 4 same-module LOWs into a focused hardening ticket (TASK-016) lets us gate TASK-013 on it cleanly. Phase-2 dev kept stack bullets in snake_case (not humanized) — defensible because humanizing+dehumanizing is irreversible for round-trip; TASK-015 can add a separate `label` field per question if pretty labels are wanted. IMPL commit body documented all three orchestrator-required decisions this time (namespacing list, YAML parser subset, BODY_SECTIONS single-source-of-truth) — clean improvement over TASK-010 phase-2 dev's empty body.

decisions.decision: TASK-018 closed APPROVE on first review pass. countFrameworkHistory aligned with backlog-seeder AC3 corruption-propagation policy via option (a). decisions.rationale: Cleanest review of the session: 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 0 LOW / 1 NIT (anchor-3 comment placement at upper bound of AC4 test window — non-blocking future-proofing concern). 191/191 vitest specs deterministic in 10.18s. Two-commit cadence: 7c400fe (test, 4 failing for the right reasons — three swallow scenarios + one missing-anchor scenario) → 222b285 (impl, +14/-6 in bin/init.js and +9/-5 in src/backlog-seeder.js). Cleanest reviewer result of the entire chain: 0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 3 LOW, 3 NIT — all polish, none blocking. 155/155 specs deterministic across two clean runs. Two architectural calls vindicated: (a) framework-history detection via inverse-of-`seed`-label heuristic survived all 6 reviewer probes including the corrupt-ticket and pre-existing-archive-clobber edges; (b) archive prompt in `created` branch only (NOT `forced`) is the right cut — separates PROJECT.md regen concerns from task-store mutation concerns. Two-ticket split keeps each follow-up scoped to one module and one reviewer's worth of context. The TASK-014 reviewer correctly flagged that the wizard's happy path doesn't trip either MEDIUM (engine pre-normalizes input), but a future non-wizard caller of seedBacklog WILL trip them — so neither is dormant in the way the TASK-013 LOWs were. decisions.decision: TASK-013 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. Generator wired into resumed branch (not just created/forced per spec) — vindicated by reviewer as defensible NIT. 3 LOW + 4 NIT findings deferred without follow-up ticket.
decisions.rationale: Impl dev shipped a wider hook surface than the ticket required — instead of only `created` and `forced` branches calling generateProjectContext, the shared runWizardAndWriteProjectMd helper covers `created`, `forced`, AND `resumed`. Dev's stated rationale ('completing intake-via-resume produces the same answers map as a fresh created branch — punching a hole there would be surprising') was independently checked by the reviewer who traced every path under which project-context.md could already exist when the resumed branch fires and found no clobber scenario reachable through normal use. The 3 LOWs (generated_at filter symmetry, TYPE_SPECIFIC_GUIDANCE drift-from-PROJECT_TYPES, unknown-type frontmatter inconsistency) are all dormant or minor — none gate TASK-014 (backlog seeder). Following the TASK-016 close pattern, deferring without a new TASK-018 polish ticket; if a TASK-015 UX-polish sprint materializes later it can sweep these in alongside the LOWs already queued from TASK-010 + TASK-012.

decisions.decision: TASK-016 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. 3 LOW + 2 NIT findings from the reviewer deferred without spawning a follow-up ticket.
decisions.rationale: All 5 ACs verified by independent reviewer (fresh context, 106/106 specs, two clean runs). The 3 LOWs are real but minor: (a) splitInlineArray strips edge whitespace per item, not lossless for items with leading/trailing spaces but writer never emits such items; (b) `formatStackValue` collapses `['']` to `[]` on disk — pre-existing asymmetry, not introduced by this ticket; (c) splitInlineArray comment description slightly off from impl behavior. The 2 NITs (unreachable lone-backslash recovery branch in decoder, test gap on `['\,', ',']` input) are even smaller. None block TASK-013, which was the trigger event TASK-016 was created to address. Spawning a TASK-017 follow-up for purely-polish findings would fragment the project-md hardening work; if they ever matter they can be folded into TASK-015 (UX polish) which already touches similar surface. AC1 escape strategy was the key locked design choice — tests-first dev picked backslash-escape over write-time-reject because the ticket explicitly required 'lossless round-trip for all wizard-generated inputs' and escape strictly dominates reject on that axis.

decisions.decision: TASK-012 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS. Dropped `hostname` parameter from runInit entirely after phase-1 dev surfaced a signature gap with startSession.
decisions.rationale: Phase-1 dev correctly flagged that `startSession` calls `hostFingerprint()` directly via `os.hostname()` — there's no plumbing point for an injected hostname. Three options surfaced: (a) extend startSession with optional hostname override (scope creep into src/lifecycle.js), (b) accept hostname in runInit and ignore (ugly), (c) drop hostname from runInit's contract (clean, YAGNI). Picked (c). Tests still pass `hostname: 'test-host'` but never assert on it; JS silently ignores the extra key. Reviewer ran in fresh context, returned 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 1 LOW / 2 NIT — cleanest review of the wizard chain so far. 98/98 specs green across two runs (deterministic). The 1 LOW (positional tokens silently ignored vs bin/new-task.js's strict throw on any unrecognized token) rolled into TASK-015 since the same UX polish pass already covers bin/new-task.js's dead-code validate and engineerPrompter typo. CLAUDE.md amendment placement verified by reviewer's offset check. The framework is now usable by a non-technical operator who clones the repo and runs `node bin/init.js` — wizard chain milestone 3 of 6 complete.
